title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market Close: May 8, 2026, and Weekend Review

items.headline: AI Rally Propels S&P 500 and Nasdaq to New Records as Investors Shrug Off Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: U.S. equities closed Friday's trading session higher, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite reaching fresh record highs, fueled by strong investor interest in artificial intelligence (AI) related stocks. The market's resilience was noted despite escalating tensions in the Middle East and rising oil prices. Strong corporate earnings and robust April jobs data further contributed to a broadly positive sentiment.

items.statContext: U.S. Markets are closed for the weekend (May 10-11, 2026).

items.headline: Nvidia Leads Chip Stocks
items.summary: Chip giant Nvidia saw gains, contributing to the broader rally in AI-linked stocks.

items.headline: Memory Firms Surge on AI Demand
items.summary: Micron Technology surged over 15% amid optimism for booming demand tied to AI data center expansion.

items.headline: Sandisk Rises on AI Optimism
items.summary: Sandisk also experienced a significant jump of over 15% due to strong demand in AI data center expansion.

items.headline: Cisco Systems Advances
items.summary: Cisco Systems was among the top performers on the Dow Jones, indicating strength in networking.

items.headline: Cloudflare Tumbles on Weak Guidance
items.summary: Cloudflare shares declined sharply following job cuts and weaker-than-expected revenue guidance.

items.headline: Trade Desk Falls on Revenue Forecast
items.summary: The Trade Desk saw a drop after providing soft revenue forecasts.

items.headline: Salesforce Declines
items.summary: Salesforce was one of the biggest decliners on Friday, reflecting broader pressures on software stocks.

items.headline: McDonald's Sees Dip
items.summary: McDonald's experienced a notable decline on Friday.

items.headline: Technology Sector Continues to Lead Rally, Semiconductors Outperform
items.summary: The Technology sector, particularly semiconductors, remained the primary driver of market gains, with the S&P 500 technology index jumping 2.7%. This extends a strong rotation into technology since late March. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index surged, extending its second-quarter gain to 55%.

items.headline: Energy Sector Becomes Laggard Despite Oil Price Rises
items.summary: While Energy was a year-to-date leader, it shifted to a laggard status over the past month. Utilities also slipped 0.9% on Friday.

items.headline: Large-Cap Software Stocks Under Pressure Amid AI Fears
items.summary: Major software stocks such as Salesforce, Intuit, Adobe, Workday, and ServiceNow have seen significant declines over the past year, as fears grow that artificial intelligence could impact demand for traditional software solutions. This contrasts with the strong performance of small-cap tech.

items.headline: Strong April Jobs Data Bolsters Expectations for Stable Fed Rates, But Hike Odds Rise
items.summary: Stronger-than-expected April jobs data, with nonfarm payrolls increasing by 115,000, reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve will likely maintain interest rates through year-end. However, futures traders are increasingly pricing in a potential Fed rate hike this year, with inflation currently at 3.3%. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) for April is due on May 12th.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Season Shows Strong Performance for S&P 500 Companies
items.summary: First-quarter earnings have largely supported equities, with 83% of the 440 S&P 500 companies reporting so far beating analysts' expectations. Leading the charge are AI-heavyweights and hyperscalers, reaffirming strong AI capital expenditure cycles.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Continue to Affect Global Energy Markets
items.summary: Renewed attacks involving U.S. and Iranian forces in the Gulf have kept Brent crude prices above US$100 per barrel amidst concerns over disruptions to oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz. Aramco announced strong Q1 2026 results, highlighting operational resilience in a complex geopolitical environment. The ongoing conflict is causing higher fuel costs and supply disruptions, particularly impacting economies in Asia.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports Expected on Monday, May 12, 2026
items.summary: Investors will be watching several companies reporting earnings on Monday, May 12th, including Talkspace ($TALK) and T1 Energy ($TE), which are scheduled to release Q1 2026 results. Cisco Systems ($CSCO) is set to report its fiscal third-quarter earnings after Wednesday's close.

items.headline: Trump-Xi Summit Looms Amidst Trade and Geopolitical Challenges
items.summary: President Donald Trump is scheduled to visit Beijing for a highly anticipated summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping next week. The discussions are expected to focus on extending the current trade truce, preserving access to U.S. technology, and addressing broader economic challenges, including a global oil shock and high compute demand.
